verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Benadad rex Syriæ congregavit omnem exercitum suum, et triginta duos reges secum, et equos, et currus : et ascendens pugnabat contra Samariam, et obsidebat eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבֶן־הֲדַ֣ד מֶלֶךְ־אֲרָ֗ם קָבַץ֙ אֶת־כׇּל־חֵיל֔וֹ וּשְׁלֹשִׁ֨ים וּשְׁנַ֥יִם מֶ֛לֶךְ אִתּ֖וֹ וְס֣וּס וָרָ֑כֶב וַיַּ֗עַל וַיָּ֙צַר֙ עַל־שֹׁ֣מְר֔וֹן וַיִּלָּ֖חֶם בָּֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Benadad, king of Syria, gathered together all his host, and there were two and thirty kings with him, and horses, and chariots: and going up, he fought against Samaria, and besieged it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Behadad Syrian kuningas kokosi kaikki sotaväkensä ja oli kaksineljättäkymmentä kuningasta hänen kanssansa, ja hevoset ja vaunut; ja hän meni ja piiritti Samarian, ja soti sitä vastaan,
verses.king_james: And Ben–hadad the king of Syria gathered all his host together: and there were thirty and two kings with him, and horses, and chariots: and he went up and besieged Samaria, and warred against it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mittensque nuntios ad Achab regem Israël in civitatem,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֧ח מַלְאָכִ֛ים אֶל־אַחְאָ֥ב מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל הָעִֽירָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And sending messengers to Achab, king of Israel, into the city,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja lähetti sanansaattajat Ahabille Israelin kuninkaalle kaupunkiin,
verses.king_james: And he sent messengers to Ahab king of Israel into the city, and said unto him, Thus saith Ben–hadad,

verses.latin_vulgate: ait : Hæc dicit Benadad : Argentum tuum, et aurum tuum meum est : et uxores tuæ, et filii tui optimi, mei sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר בֶּן־הֲדַ֔ד כַּסְפְּךָ֥ וּֽזְהָבְךָ֖ לִי־ה֑וּא וְנָשֶׁ֧יךָ וּבָנֶ֛יךָ הַטּוֹבִ֖ים לִי־הֵֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said: Thus saith Benadad: Thy silver and thy gold is mine: and thy wives and thy goodliest children are mine.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja käski hänelle sanottaa: näin sanoo Benhadad: sinun hopias ja kultas ovat minun, ja sinun emäntäs ja ihanimmat lapses ovat myös minun.
verses.king_james: Thy silver and thy gold is mine; thy wives also and thy children, even the goodliest, are mine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responditque rex Israël : Juxta verbum tuum, domine mi rex, tuus sum ego, et omnia mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַן מֶֽלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ וַיֹּ֔אמֶר כִּדְבָרְךָ֖ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ לְךָ֥ אֲנִ֖י וְכׇל־אֲשֶׁר־לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel answered: According to thy word, my lord, O king, I am thine, and all that I have.
verses.finnish_biblia: Israelin kuningas vastasi ja sanoi: herrani kuningas, niinkuin sinä sanonut olet, minä olen sinun ja kaikki mitä minulla on.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel answered and said, My lord, O king, according to thy saying, I am thine, and all that I have.

verses.latin_vulgate: Revertentesque nuntii, dixerunt : Hæc dicit Benadad, qui misit nos ad te : Argentum tuum, et aurum tuum, et uxores tuas, et filios tuos, dabis mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּשֻׁ֙בוּ֙ הַמַּלְאָכִ֔ים וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ כֹּה־אָמַ֥ר בֶּן־הֲדַ֖ד לֵאמֹ֑ר כִּֽי־שָׁלַ֤חְתִּי אֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר כַּסְפְּךָ֧ וּֽזְהָבְךָ֛ וְנָשֶׁ֥יךָ וּבָנֶ֖יךָ לִ֥י תִתֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the messengers came again, and said: Thus saith Benadad, who sent us unto thee: Thy silver and thy gold, and thy wives and thy children, thou shalt deliver up to me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanansaattajat tulivat jälleen ja sanoivat: näin sanoo Benhadad: minä olen lähettänyt sinun tykös sanan, sanoen: sinun hopias ja kultas, emäntäs ja lapses pitää sinun minulle antaman:
verses.king_james: And the messengers came again, and said, Thus speaketh Ben–hadad, saying, Although I have sent unto thee, saying, Thou shalt deliver me thy silver, and thy gold, and thy wives, and thy children;

verses.latin_vulgate: Cras igitur hac eadem hora mittam servos meos ad te, et scrutabuntur domum tuam, et domum servorum tuorum : et omne quod eis placuerit, ponent in manibus suis, et auferent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י ׀ אִם־כָּעֵ֣ת מָחָ֗ר אֶשְׁלַ֤ח אֶת־עֲבָדַי֙ אֵלֶ֔יךָ וְחִפְּשׂוּ֙ אֶת־בֵּ֣יתְךָ֔ וְאֵ֖ת בָּתֵּ֣י עֲבָדֶ֑יךָ וְהָיָה֙ כׇּל־מַחְמַ֣ד עֵינֶ֔יךָ יָשִׂ֥ימוּ בְיָדָ֖ם וְלָקָֽחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: To morrow, therefore, at this same hour, I will send my servants to thee, and they shall search thy house, and the houses of thy servants: and all that pleaseth them, they shall put in their hands, and take away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minä huomenna tällä aikaa lähetän palveliani sinun tykös tutkimaan sinun huonees ja sinun palvelias huoneet: ja mitä sinulla rakkainta on, pitää heidän ottaman ja tuoman tänne.
verses.king_james: Yet I will send my servants unto thee to morrow about this time, and they shall search thine house, and the houses of thy servants; and it shall be, that whatsoever is pleasant in thine eyes, they shall put it in their hand, and take it away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocavit autem rex Israël omnes seniores terræ, et ait : Animadvertite, et videte quoniam insidietur nobis : misit enim ad me pro uxoribus meis, et filiis, et pro argento et auro : et non abnui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֤א מֶֽלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ לְכׇל־זִקְנֵ֣י הָאָ֔רֶץ וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ דְּעוּ־נָ֣א וּרְא֔וּ כִּ֥י רָעָ֖ה זֶ֣ה מְבַקֵּ֑שׁ כִּֽי־שָׁלַ֨ח אֵלַ֜י לְנָשַׁ֤י וּלְבָנַי֙ וּלְכַסְפִּ֣י וְלִזְהָבִ֔י וְלֹ֥א מָנַ֖עְתִּי מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel called all the ancients of the land, and said: Mark, and see that he layeth snares for us. For he sent to me for my wives, and for my children, and for my silver and gold: and I said not nay.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Israelin kuningas kutsui kaikki maan vanhimmat ja sanoi: tutkikaat ja katsokaat, mitä pahuutta on hänen mielessänsä; sillä hän lähetti sanan minun tyköni, minun emännistäni ja lapsistani, hopiastani ja kullastani, ja en ole minä häneltä kieltänyt.
verses.king_james: Then the king of Israel called all the elders of the land, and said, Mark, I pray you, and see how this man seeketh mischief: for he sent unto me for my wives, and for my children, and for my silver, and for my gold; and I denied him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixeruntque omnes majores natu, et universus populus, ad eum : Non audias, neque acquiescas illi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֥וּ אֵלָ֛יו כׇּל־הַזְּקֵנִ֖ים וְכׇל־הָעָ֑ם אַל־תִּשְׁמַ֖ע וְל֥וֹא תֹאבֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all the ancients, and all the people said to him: Hearken not to him, nor consent to him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoivat hänelle kaikki vanhimmat ja kaikki kansa: ei sinun pidä häntä kuuleman, eikä häneen suostuman.
verses.king_james: And all the elders and all the people said unto him, Hearken not unto him, nor consent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit itaque nuntiis Benadad : Dicite domino meo regi : Omnia propter quæ misisti ad me servum tuum in initio, faciam : hanc autem rem facere non possum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֜אמֶר לְמַלְאֲכֵ֣י בֶן־הֲדַ֗ד אִמְר֞וּ לַאדֹנִ֤י הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ כֹּל֩ אֲשֶׁר־שָׁלַ֨חְתָּ אֶל־עַבְדְּךָ֤ בָרִֽאשֹׁנָה֙ אֶעֱשֶׂ֔ה וְהַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה לֹ֥א אוּכַ֖ל לַעֲשׂ֑וֹת וַיֵּֽלְכוּ֙ הַמַּלְאָכִ֔ים וַיְשִׁבֻ֖הוּ דָּבָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore he answered the messengers of Benadad: Tell my lord, the king: All that thou didst send for to me, thy servant at first, I will do: but this thing I cannot do.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi Benhadadin sanansaattajille: sanokaat herralleni kuninkaalle: kaikki minkä hän käski minulle palveliallensa ensin, teen minä, mutta tätä en minä taida tehdä. Ja sanansaattajat menivät takaperin ja sanoivat vastauksen hänelle.
verses.king_james: Wherefore he said unto the messengers of Ben–hadad, Tell my lord the king, All that thou didst send for to thy servant at the first I will do: but this thing I may not do. And the messengers departed, and brought him word again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversique nuntii retulerunt ei. Qui remisit, et ait : Hæc faciant mihi dii, et hæc addant, si suffecerit pulvis Samariæ pugillis omnis populi qui sequitur me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח אֵלָיו֙ בֶּן־הֲדַ֔ד וַיֹּ֕אמֶר כֹּה־יַעֲשׂ֥וּן לִ֛י אֱלֹהִ֖ים וְכֹ֣ה יוֹסִ֑פוּ אִם־יִשְׂפֹּק֙ עֲפַ֣ר שֹׁמְר֔וֹן לִשְׁעָלִ֕ים לְכׇל־הָעָ֖ם אֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּרַגְלָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the messengers returning brought him word. And he sent again, and said: Such and such things may the gods do to me, and more may they add, if the dust of Samaria shall suffice for handfuls for all the people that follow me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte lähetti Benhadad hänen tykönsä ja käski hänelle sanoa: jumalat tehköön minulle niin ja niin, jos multa Samariassa piisaa, että kaikki minua seuraava kansa taitais siitä ottaa pivonsa täyden.
verses.king_james: And Ben–hadad sent unto him, and said, The gods do so unto me, and more also, if the dust of Samaria shall suffice for handfuls for all the people that follow me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens rex Israël, ait : Dicite ei : Ne glorietur, accinctus æque ut discinctus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַן מֶֽלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דַּבְּר֔וּ אַל־יִתְהַלֵּ֥ל חֹגֵ֖ר כִּמְפַתֵּֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel answering, said: Tell him: Let not the girded boast himself as the ungirded.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Israelin kuningas vastasi ja sanoi: sanokaat: joka vyöttää itsensä, älkään kerskatko, niinkuin se joka riisuu.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel answered and said, Tell him, Let not him that girdeth on his harness boast himself as he that putteth it off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem cum audisset Benadad verbum istud, bibebat ipse et reges in umbraculis : et ait servis suis : Circumdate civitatem. Et circumdederunt eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י כִּשְׁמֹ֙עַ֙ אֶת־הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה וְה֥וּא שֹׁתֶ֛ה ה֥וּא וְהַמְּלָכִ֖ים בַּסֻּכּ֑וֹת וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֶל־עֲבָדָיו֙ שִׂ֔ימוּ וַיָּשִׂ֖ימוּ עַל־הָעִֽיר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass, when Benadad heard this word, that he and the kings were drinking in pavilions, and he said to his servants: Beset the city. And they beset it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin hän sen kuuli, kuningasten kanssa juodessansa majassa, sanoi hän palvelioillensa: valmistakaat teitänne. Ja he valmistavat heitänsä kaupunkia vastaan.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Ben–hadad heard this message, as he was drinking, he and the kings in the pavilions, that he said unto his servants, Set yourselves in array. And they set themselves in array against the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce propheta unus accedens ad Achab regem Israël, ait ei : Hæc dicit Dominus : Certe vidisti omnem multitudinem hanc nimiam ? ecce ego tradam eam in manu tua hodie, ut scias quia ego sum Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִנֵּ֣ה ׀ נָבִ֣יא אֶחָ֗ד נִגַּשׁ֮ אֶל־אַחְאָ֣ב מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵל֒ וַיֹּ֗אמֶר כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הֲֽרָאִ֔יתָ אֵ֛ת כׇּל־הֶהָמ֥וֹן הַגָּד֖וֹל הַזֶּ֑ה הִנְנִ֨י נֹתְנ֤וֹ בְיָֽדְךָ֙ הַיּ֔וֹם וְיָדַעְתָּ֖ כִּֽי־אֲנִ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And behold a prophet coming to Achab, king of Israel, said to him: Thus saith the Lord: Hast thou seen all this exceeding great multitude? behold I will deliver them into thy hand this day: that thou mayst know that I am the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, yksi propheta astui Ahabin Israelin kuninkaan tykö ja sanoi: näin sanoo Herra; etkös nähnyt kaikkea sitä suurta joukkoa? Katso, minä annan heidät sinun käsiis tänäpänä, että sinun pitää tietämän, että minä olen Herra.
verses.king_james: And, behold, there came a prophet unto Ahab king of Israel, saying, Thus saith the Lord, Hast thou seen all this great multitude? behold, I will deliver it into thine hand this day; and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Achab : Per quem ? Dixitque ei : Hæc dicit Dominus : Per pedissequos principum provinciarum. Et ait : Quis incipiet præliari ? Et ille dixit : Tu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אַחְאָב֙ בְּמִ֔י וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ כֹּה־אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה בְּנַעֲרֵ֖י שָׂרֵ֣י הַמְּדִינ֑וֹת וַיֹּ֛אמֶר מִי־יֶאְסֹ֥ר הַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה וַיֹּ֥אמֶר אָֽתָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Achab said: By whom? And he said to him: Thus saith the Lord: By the servants of the princes of the provinces. And he said: Who shall begin to fight? And he said: Thou.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ahab sanoi: kenen kautta? Ja hän sanoi: näin sanoo Herra: maan ruhtinasten palveliain kautta: Hän sanoi: kuka alkaa sodan? Hän vastasi: sinä.
verses.king_james: And Ahab said, By whom? And he said, Thus saith the Lord, Even by the young men of the princes of the provinces. Then he said, Who shall order the battle? And he answered, Thou.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recensuit ergo pueros principum provinciarum, et reperit numerum ducentorum triginta duorum : et recensuit post eos populum, omnes filios Israël, septem millia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּפְקֹ֗ד אֶֽת־נַעֲרֵי֙ שָׂרֵ֣י הַמְּדִינ֔וֹת וַיִּהְי֕וּ מָאתַ֖יִם שְׁנַ֣יִם וּשְׁלֹשִׁ֑ים וְאַחֲרֵיהֶ֗ם פָּקַ֧ד אֶת־כׇּל־הָעָ֛ם כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל שִׁבְעַ֥ת אֲלָפִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: So he mustered the servants of the princes of the provinces, and he found the number of two hundred and thirty-two: and he mustered after them the people, all the children of Israel, seven thousand:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän luki maan ruhtinasten palveliat, ja heitä oli kaksisataa ja kaksineljättäkymmentä. Ja niiden jälkeen luki hän kaiken kansan jokaisesta Israelin lapsista, seitsemäntuhatta miestä.
verses.king_james: Then he numbered the young men of the princes of the provinces, and they were two hundred and thirty two: and after them he numbered all the people, even all the children of Israel, being seven thousand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressi sunt meridie. Benadad autem bibebat temulentus in umbraculo suo, et reges triginta duo cum eo, qui ad auxilium ejus venerant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצְא֖וּ בַּֽצׇּהֳרָ֑יִם וּבֶן־הֲדַד֩ שֹׁתֶ֨ה שִׁכּ֜וֹר בַּסֻּכּ֗וֹת ה֧וּא וְהַמְּלָכִ֛ים שְׁלֹשִֽׁים־וּשְׁנַ֥יִם מֶ֖לֶךְ עֹזֵ֥ר אֹתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they went out at noon. But Benadad was drinking himself drunk in his pavilion, and the two and thirty kings with him, who were come to help him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he läksivät ulos puolipäivästä; mutta Benhadad oli juopuneena majassa kahdenneljättäkymmentä kuninkaan kanssa, jotka häntä auttamaan tulivat.
verses.king_james: And they went out at noon. But Ben–hadad was drinking himself drunk in the pavilions, he and the kings, the thirty and two kings that helped him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressi sunt autem pueri principum provinciarum in prima fronte. Misit itaque Benadad : qui nuntiaverunt ei, dicentes : Viri egressi sunt de Samaria.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצְא֗וּ נַעֲרֵ֛י שָׂרֵ֥י הַמְּדִינ֖וֹת בָּרִֽאשֹׁנָ֑ה וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח בֶּן־הֲדַ֗ד וַיַּגִּ֤ידוּ לוֹ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר אֲנָשִׁ֕ים יָצְא֖וּ מִשֹּׁמְרֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the servants of the princes of the provinces went out first. And Benadad sent. And they told him, saying: There are men come out of Samaria.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja maan ruhtinasten palveliat läksivät ulos ensin; mutta Benhadad lähetti, ja he ilmoittivat hänelle, sanoen: miehet lähtevät ulos Samariasta.
verses.king_james: And the young men of the princes of the provinces went out first; and Ben–hadad sent out, and they told him, saying, There are men come out of Samaria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ille ait : Sive pro pace veniunt, apprehendite eos vivos : sive ut prælientur, vivos eos capite.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֛אמֶר אִם־לְשָׁל֥וֹם יָצָ֖אוּ תִּפְשׂ֣וּם חַיִּ֑ים וְאִ֧ם לְמִלְחָמָ֛ה יָצָ֖אוּ חַיִּ֥ים תִּפְשֽׂוּם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: Whether they come for peace, take them alive: or whether they come to fight, take them alive.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: joko he ovat rauhan tähden uloslähteneet, niin käsittäkäät heitä elävinä, eli jos he ovat lähteneet sotaan, niin ottakaat heitä myös elävinä kiinni.
verses.king_james: And he said, Whether they be come out for peace, take them alive; or whether they be come out for war, take them alive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressi sunt ergo pueri principum provinciarum, ac reliquus exercitus sequebatur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֵ֙לֶּה֙ יָצְא֣וּ מִן־הָעִ֔יר נַעֲרֵ֖י שָׂרֵ֣י הַמְּדִינ֑וֹת וְהַחַ֖יִל אֲשֶׁ֥ר אַחֲרֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the servants of the princes of the provinces went out, and the rest of the army followed:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin maan ruhtinasten palveliat olivat lähteneet kaupungista ja sotaväki heidän perässänsä.
verses.king_james: So these young men of the princes of the provinces came out of the city, and the army which followed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: et percussit unusquisque virum qui contra se veniebat : fugeruntque Syri, et persecutus est eos Israël. Fugit quoque Benadad rex Syriæ in equo cum equitibus suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכּוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ אִישׁ֔וֹ וַיָּנֻ֣סֽוּ אֲרָ֔ם וַֽיִּרְדְּפֵ֖ם יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל וַיִּמָּלֵ֗ט בֶּן־הֲדַד֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ אֲרָ֔ם עַל־ס֖וּס וּפָרָשִֽׁים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And every one slew the man that came against him: and the Syrians fled, and Israel pursued after them. And Benadad, king of Syria, fled away on horseback with his horsemen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin löi mies miehensä ja Syrialaiset pakenivat, ja Israel ajoi heitä takaa; ja Benhadad Syrian kuningas pääsi hevostensa selkään hevosmiestensä kanssa.
verses.king_james: And they slew every one his man: and the Syrians fled; and Israel pursued them: and Ben–hadad the king of Syria escaped on an horse with the horsemen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Necnon egressus rex Israël percussit equos et currus, et percussit Syriam plaga magna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵא֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיַּ֥ךְ אֶת־הַסּ֖וּס וְאֶת־הָרָ֑כֶב וְהִכָּ֥ה בַאֲרָ֖ם מַכָּ֥ה גְדוֹלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the king of Israel going out overthrew the horses and chariots, and slew the Syrians with a great slaughter.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Israelin kuningas läksi ulos ja löi hevosia ja vaunuja; ja hän tappoi sangen paljo Syrialaisia.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel went out, and smote the horses and chariots, and slew the Syrians with a great slaughter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accedens autem propheta ad regem Israël, dixit ei : Vade, et confortare, et scito, et vide quid facias : sequenti enim anno rex Syriæ ascendet contra te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגַּ֤שׁ הַנָּבִיא֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ לֵ֣ךְ הִתְחַזַּ֔ק וְדַ֥ע וּרְאֵ֖ה אֵ֣ת אֲשֶׁר־תַּעֲשֶׂ֑ה כִּ֚י לִתְשׁוּבַ֣ת הַשָּׁנָ֔ה מֶ֥לֶךְ אֲרָ֖ם עֹלֶ֥ה עָלֶֽיךָ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: (And a prophet coming to the king of Israel, said to him: Go, and strengthen thyself, and know, and see what thou dost: for the next year the king of Syria will come up against thee.)
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin astui propheta Israelin kuninkaan tykö ja sanoi hänelle: mene ja vahvista sinus, ymmärrä ja katso, mitä sinun tekemän pitää; sillä Syrian kuningas on sinua vastaan nouseva jälleen tämän vuoden perästä.
verses.king_james: And the prophet came to the king of Israel, and said unto him, Go, strengthen thyself, and mark, and see what thou doest: for at the return of the year the king of Syria will come up against thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Servi vero regis Syriæ dixerunt ei : Dii montium sunt dii eorum, ideo superaverunt nos : sed melius est ut pugnemus contra eos in campestribus, et obtinebimus eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַבְדֵ֨י מֶלֶךְ־אֲרָ֜ם אָמְר֣וּ אֵלָ֗יו אֱלֹהֵ֤י הָרִים֙ אֱלֹ֣הֵיהֶ֔ם עַל־כֵּ֖ן חָזְק֣וּ מִמֶּ֑נּוּ וְאוּלָ֗ם נִלָּחֵ֤ם אִתָּם֙ בַּמִּישׁ֔וֹר אִם־לֹ֥א נֶחֱזַ֖ק מֵהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the servants of the king of Syria said to him: Their gods are gods of the hills, therefore they have overcome us: but it is better that we should fight against them in the plains, and we shall overcome them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Syrian kuninkaan palveliat sanoivat hänelle; heidän jumalansa ovat vuorten jumalat, sentähden voittivat he meidät: jos me vielä heitä vastaan kedolla sodimme, mitämaks me voitamme heidät.
verses.king_james: And the servants of the king of Syria said unto him, Their gods are gods of the hills; therefore they were stronger than we; but let us fight against them in the plain, and surely we shall be stronger than they.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu ergo verbum hoc fac : amove reges singulos ab exercitu tuo, et pone principes pro eis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־הַדָּבָ֥ר הַזֶּ֖ה עֲשֵׂ֑ה הָסֵ֤ר הַמְּלָכִים֙ אִ֣ישׁ מִמְּקֹמ֔וֹ וְשִׂ֥ים פַּח֖וֹת תַּחְתֵּיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Do thou, therefore, this thing: Remove all the kings from thy army, and put captains in their stead:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tee nyt tämä: ota kaikki kuninkaat pois heidän sioistansa ja pane ruhtinaat heidän siaansa;
verses.king_james: And do this thing, Take the kings away, every man out of his place, and put captains in their rooms:

verses.latin_vulgate: et instaura numerum militum qui ceciderunt de tuis, et equos secundum equos pristinos, et currus secundum currus quos ante habuisti : et pugnabimus contra eos in campestribus, et videbis quod obtinebimus eos. Credidit consilio eorum, et fecit ita.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתָּ֣ה תִֽמְנֶֽה־לְךָ֣ ׀ חַ֡יִל כַּחַ֩יִל֩ הַנֹּפֵ֨ל מֵאוֹתָ֜ךְ וְס֥וּס כַּסּ֣וּס ׀ וְרֶ֣כֶב כָּרֶ֗כֶב וְנִֽלָּחֲמָ֤ה אוֹתָם֙ בַּמִּישׁ֔וֹר אִם־לֹ֥א נֶחֱזַ֖ק מֵהֶ֑ם וַיִּשְׁמַ֥ע לְקֹלָ֖ם וַיַּ֥עַשׂ כֵּֽן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And make up the number of soldiers that have been slain of thine, and horses, according to the former horses, and chariots, according to the chariots which thou hadst before: and we will fight against them in the plains, and thou shalt see that we shall overcome them. He believed their counsel, and did so.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja aseta sinulles sotajoukko, senkaltainen kuin tämä sotajoukko oli, ja hevoset ja vaunut niinkuin ennenkin, ja me sodimme heitä vastaan kedolla, mitämaks me voitamme heidät. Ja hän kuuli heitä ja teki niin.
verses.king_james: And number thee an army, like the army that thou hast lost, horse for horse, and chariot for chariot: and we will fight against them in the plain, and surely we shall be stronger than they. And he hearkened unto their voice, and did so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur postquam annus transierat, recensuit Benadad Syros, et ascendit in Aphec ut pugnaret contra Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ לִתְשׁוּבַ֣ת הַשָּׁנָ֔ה וַיִּפְקֹ֥ד בֶּן־הֲדַ֖ד אֶת־אֲרָ֑ם וַיַּ֣עַל אֲפֵ֔קָה לַמִּלְחָמָ֖ה עִם־יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Wherefore, at the return of the year, Benadad mustered the Syrians, and went up to Aphec, to fight against Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin ajastaika kulunut oli, asetti Benhadad Syrialaiset, ja meni ylös Aphekiin, sotimaan Israelia vastaan.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass at the return of the year, that Ben–hadad numbered the Syrians, and went up to Aphek, to fight against Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro filii Israël recensiti sunt, et acceptis cibariis profecti ex adverso, castraque metati sunt contra eos, quasi duo parvi greges caprarum : Syri autem repleverunt terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְנֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל הׇתְפָּקְדוּ֙ וְכׇלְכְּל֔וּ וַיֵּלְכ֖וּ לִקְרָאתָ֑ם וַיַּחֲנ֨וּ בְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל נֶגְדָּ֗ם כִּשְׁנֵי֙ חֲשִׂפֵ֣י עִזִּ֔ים וַאֲרָ֖ם מִלְא֥וּ אֶת־הָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the children of Israel were mustered, and taking victuals, went out on the other side, and encamped over against them, like two little flocks of goats: but the Syrians filled the land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Israelin lapset nousivat myös ja hankitsivat itsensä ja menivät heitä vastaan; ja Israelin lapset sioittivat itsensä heidän kohdallensa, niinkuin kaksi vähää vuohilaumaa; mutta maa oli täynnä Syrialaisia.
verses.king_james: And the children of Israel were numbered, and were all present, and went against them: and the children of Israel pitched before them like two little flocks of kids; but the Syrians filled the country.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Et accedens unus vir Dei, dixit ad regem Israël : Hæc dicit Dominus : Quia dixerunt Syri : Deus montium est Dominus, et non est Deus vallium : dabo omnem multitudinem hanc grandem in manu tua, et scietis quia ego sum Dominus.)
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגַּ֞שׁ אִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֗ים וַיֹּ֘אמֶר֮ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵל֒ וַיֹּ֜אמֶר כֹּֽה־אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה יַ֠עַן אֲשֶׁ֨ר אָמְר֤וּ אֲרָם֙ אֱלֹהֵ֤י הָרִים֙ יְהֹוָ֔ה וְלֹֽא־אֱלֹהֵ֥י עֲמָקִ֖ים ה֑וּא וְ֠נָתַתִּ֠י אֶת־כׇּל־הֶהָמ֨וֹן הַגָּד֤וֹל הַזֶּה֙ בְּיָדֶ֔ךָ וִידַעְתֶּ֖ם כִּֽי־אֲנִ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: (And a man of God coming, said to the king of Israel: Thus saith the Lord: Because the Syrians have said: The Lord is God of the hills, but is not God of the valleys: I will deliver all this great multitude into thy hand, and you shall know that I am the Lord.)
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jumalan mies astui edes ja sanoi Israelin kuninkaalle: näin sanoo Herra: että Syrialaiset ovat sanoneet: Herra on vuorten Jumala ja ei laaksoin Jumala, niin minä tahdon antaa kaiken tämän suuren joukon sinun kätees, että tietäisitte minun olevan Herran.
verses.king_james: And there came a man of God, and spake unto the king of Israel, and said, Thus saith the Lord, Because the Syrians have said, The Lord is God of the hills, but he is not God of the valleys, therefore will I deliver all this great multitude into thine hand, and ye shall know that I am the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dirigebantque septem diebus ex adverso hi atque illi acies, septima autem die commissum est bellum : percusseruntque filii Israël de Syris centum millia peditum in die una.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיַּחֲנ֧וּ אֵ֦לֶּה נֹֽכַח־אֵ֖לֶּה שִׁבְעַ֣ת יָמִ֑ים וַיְהִ֣י ׀ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַשְּׁבִיעִ֗י וַתִּקְרַב֙ הַמִּלְחָמָ֔ה וַיַּכּ֨וּ בְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵ֧ל אֶת־אֲרָ֛ם מֵאָה־אֶ֥לֶף רַגְלִ֖י בְּי֥וֹם אֶחָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: And both sides set their armies in array one against the other seven days, and on the seventh day the battle was fought: and the children of Israel slew, of the Syrians, a hundred thousand footmen in one day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he asettivat leirinsä toistensa kohdalle seitsemäksi päiväksi, mutta seitsemäntenä päivänä he menivät sotimaan yhteen. Ja Israelin lapset löivät Syrialaisia satatuhatta jalkamiestä yhtenä päivänä.
verses.king_james: And they pitched one over against the other seven days. And so it was, that in the seventh day the battle was joined: and the children of Israel slew of the Syrians an hundred thousand footmen in one day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugerunt autem qui remanserant in Aphec, in civitatem : et cecidit murus super viginti septem millia hominum qui remanserant. Porro Benadad fugiens ingressus est civitatem, in cubiculum quod erat intra cubiculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּנֻ֨סוּ הַנּוֹתָרִ֥ים ׀ אֲפֵ֘קָה֮ אֶל־הָעִיר֒ וַתִּפֹּל֙ הַחוֹמָ֔ה עַל־עֶשְׂרִ֨ים וְשִׁבְעָ֥ה אֶ֛לֶף אִ֖ישׁ הַנּוֹתָרִ֑ים וּבֶן־הֲדַ֣ד נָ֔ס וַיָּבֹ֥א אֶל־הָעִ֖יר חֶ֥דֶר בְּחָֽדֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they that remained fled to Aphec, into the city: and the wall fell upon seven and twenty thousand men, that were left. And Benadad fleeing, went into the city, into a chamber that was within a chamber.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jääneet pakenivat Aphekin kaupunkiin. Ja muuri kaatui seitsemänkolmattakymmenen tuhannen miehen päälle, jotka jääneet olivat. Ja Benhadad pakeni myös kaupunkiin, yhdestä majasta toiseen.
verses.king_james: But the rest fled to Aphek, into the city; and there a wall fell upon twenty and seven thousand of the men that were left. And Ben–hadad fled, and came into the city, into an inner chamber.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixeruntque ei servi sui : Ecce, audivimus quod reges domus Israël clementes sint : ponamus itaque saccos in lumbis nostris, et funiculos in capitibus nostris, et egrediamur ad regem Israël : forsitan salvabit animas nostras.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֵלָיו֮ עֲבָדָיו֒ הִנֵּה־נָ֣א שָׁמַ֔עְנוּ כִּ֗י מַלְכֵי֙ בֵּ֣ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כִּֽי־מַלְכֵ֥י חֶ֖סֶד הֵ֑ם נָשִׂ֣ימָה נָּא֩ שַׂקִּ֨ים בְּמׇתְנֵ֜ינוּ וַחֲבָלִ֣ים בְּרֹאשֵׁ֗נוּ וְנֵצֵא֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אוּלַ֖י יְחַיֶּ֥ה אֶת־נַפְשֶֽׁךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And his servants said to him: Behold, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful; so let us put sackcloths on our loins, and ropes on our heads, and go out to the king of Israel: perhaps he will save our lives.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoivat hänen palveliansa hänelle: katso, me olemme kuulleet Israelin huoneen kuninkaat olevan laupiaat kuninkaat: niin pankaamme säkit kupeisiimme ja köydet päihimme ja menkäämme Israelin kuninkaan tykö, kuka tiesi että hän sallii sinun sielus elää.
verses.king_james: And his servants said unto him, Behold now, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings: let us, I pray thee, put sackcloth on our loins, and ropes upon our heads, and go out to the king of Israel: peradventure he will save thy life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accinxerunt saccis lumbos suos, et posuerunt funiculos in capitibus suis, veneruntque ad regem Israël, et dixerunt ei : Servus tuus Benadad dicit : Vivat, oro te, anima mea. Et ille ait : Si adhuc vivit, frater meus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּחְגְּרוּ֩ שַׂקִּ֨ים בְּמׇתְנֵיהֶ֜ם וַחֲבָלִ֣ים בְּרָאשֵׁיהֶ֗ם וַיָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וַיֹּ֣אמְר֔וּ עַבְדְּךָ֧ בֶן־הֲדַ֛ד אָמַ֖ר תְּחִי־נָ֣א נַפְשִׁ֑י וַיֹּ֛אמֶר הַעוֹדֶ֥נּוּ חַ֖י אָחִ֥י הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: So they girded sackcloths on their loins, and put ropes on their heads, and came to the king of Israel, and said to him: Thy servant, Benadad, saith: I beseech thee let me have my life. And he said: If he be yet alive, he is my brother.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he panivat säkit kupeisiinsa ja köydet päihinsä ja tulivat Israelin kuninkaan tykö, ja sanoivat: Benhadad palvelias sanoo sinulle: minä rukoilen, salli minun sieluni elää. Ja hän sanoi: vieläkö hän elää? hän on minun veljeni.
verses.king_james: So they girded sackcloth on their loins, and put ropes on their heads, and came to the king of Israel, and said, Thy servant Ben–hadad saith, I pray thee, let me live. And he said, Is he yet alive? he is my brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod acceperunt viri pro omine : et festinantes rapuerunt verbum ex ore ejus, atque dixerunt : Frater tuus Benadad. Et dixit eis : Ite, et adducite eum ad me. Egressus est ergo ad eum Benadad, et levavit eum in currum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָאֲנָשִׁים֩ יְנַחֲשׁ֨וּ וַֽיְמַהֲר֜וּ וַיַּחְלְט֣וּ הֲמִמֶּ֗נּוּ וַיֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ אָחִ֣יךָ בֶן־הֲדַ֔ד וַיֹּ֖אמֶר בֹּ֣אוּ קָחֻ֑הוּ וַיֵּצֵ֤א אֵלָיו֙ בֶּן־הֲדַ֔ד וַֽיַּעֲלֵ֖הוּ עַל־הַמֶּרְכָּבָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The men took this for good luck: and in haste caught the word out of his mouth, and said: Thy brother Benadad. And he said to them: Go, and bring him to me. Then Benadad came out to him, and he lifted him up into his chariot.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ne miehet ottivat kiinni sen sanan häneltä nopiasti ja käänsivät itsellensä hyväksi, ja sanoivat: sinun veljes Benhadad: niin hän sanoi: menkäät ja johdattakaat häntä tänne. Niin Benhadad meni hänen tykönsä, ja hän antoi hänen istua vaunuun.
verses.king_james: Now the men did diligently observe whether any thing would come from him, and did hastily catch it: and they said, Thy brother Ben–hadad. Then he said, Go ye, bring him. Then Ben–hadad came forth to him; and he caused him to come up into the chariot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui dixit ei : Civitates quas tulit pater meus a patre tuo, reddam : et plateas fac tibi in Damasco, sicut fecit pater meus in Samaria, et ego fœderatus recedam a te. Pepigit ergo fœdus, et dimisit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֡יו הֶעָרִ֣ים אֲשֶׁר־לָֽקַח־אָבִי֩ מֵאֵ֨ת אָבִ֜יךָ אָשִׁ֗יב וְ֠חֻצ֠וֹת תָּשִׂ֨ים לְךָ֤ בְדַמֶּ֙שֶׂק֙ כַּֽאֲשֶׁר־שָׂ֤ם אָבִי֙ בְּשֹׁ֣מְר֔וֹן וַאֲנִ֖י בַּבְּרִ֣ית אֲשַׁלְּחֶ֑ךָּ וַיִּכְרׇת־ל֥וֹ בְרִ֖ית וַֽיְשַׁלְּחֵֽהוּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to him: The cities which my father took from thy father, I will restore: and do thou make thee streets in Damascus, as my father made in Samaria and having made a league, I will depart from thee. So he made a league with him, and let him go.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi hänelle: ne kaupungit, jota minun isäni sinun isältäs ottanut on, annan minä jälleen, ja tee kadut sinulles Damaskuun, niinkuin minun isäni teki Samariassa: niin minä teen liiton sinun kanssas ja annan sinun mennä. Ja hän teki liiton hänen kanssansa ja päästi hänen menemään.
verses.king_james: And Ben–hadad said unto him, The cities, which my father took from thy father, I will restore; and thou shalt make streets for thee in Damascus, as my father made in Samaria. Then said Ahab, I will send thee away with this covenant. So he made a covenant with him, and sent him away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc vir quidam de filiis prophetarum dixit ad socium suum in sermone Domini : Percute me. At ille noluit percutere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֨ישׁ אֶחָ֜ד מִבְּנֵ֣י הַנְּבִיאִ֗ים אָמַ֧ר אֶל־רֵעֵ֛הוּ בִּדְבַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה הַכֵּ֣ינִי נָ֑א וַיְמָאֵ֥ן הָאִ֖ישׁ לְהַכֹּתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then a certain man of the sons of the prophets, said to his companion, in the word of the Lord: Strike me. But he would not strike.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin sanoi yksi mies prophetan lapsista lähimmäisellensä Herran sanan kautta: lyö minua! vaan hän kielsi, ettei hän häntä lyönyt.
verses.king_james: And a certain man of the sons of the prophets said unto his neighbour in the word of the Lord, Smite me, I pray thee. And the man refused to smite him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui ait : Quia noluisti audire vocem Domini, ecce recedes a me, et percutiet te leo. Cumque paululum recessisset ab eo, invenit eum leo, atque percussit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֗וֹ יַ֚עַן אֲשֶׁ֤ר לֹא־שָׁמַ֙עְתָּ֙ בְּק֣וֹל יְהֹוָ֔ה הִנְּךָ֤ הוֹלֵךְ֙ מֵֽאִתִּ֔י וְהִכְּךָ֖ הָאַרְיֵ֑ה וַיֵּ֙לֶךְ֙ מֵֽאֶצְל֔וֹ וַיִּמְצָאֵ֥הוּ הָאַרְיֵ֖ה וַיַּכֵּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then he said to him: Because thou wouldst not hearken to the word of the Lord, behold thou shalt depart from me, and a lion shall slay thee. And when he was gone a little from him, a lion found him, and slew him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän sanoi hänelle jällensä: ettet sinä kuullut Herran ääntä, katso, koskas lähdet minun tyköäni, niin jalopeura lyö sinun. Ja kuin hän läksi hänen tyköänsä, kohtasi hänen jalopeura ja löi hänen.
verses.king_james: Then said he unto him, Because thou hast not obeyed the voice of the Lord, behold, as soon as thou art departed from me, a lion shall slay thee. And as soon as he was departed from him, a lion found him, and slew him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et alterum inveniens virum, dixit ad eum : Percute me. Qui percussit eum, et vulneravit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּמְצָא֙ אִ֣ישׁ אַחֵ֔ר וַיֹּ֖אמֶר הַכֵּ֣ינִי נָ֑א וַיַּכֵּ֥הוּ הָאִ֖ישׁ הַכֵּ֥ה וּפָצֹֽעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then he found another man, and said to him: Strike me. And he struck him and wounded him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän löysi toisen miehen ja sanoi: lyö minua! Ja se mies löi häntä kovasti ja haavoitti hänen.
verses.king_james: Then he found another man, and said, Smite me, I pray thee. And the man smote him, so that in smiting he wounded him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit ergo propheta, et occurrit regi in via, et mutavit aspersione pulveris os et oculos suos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֙לֶךְ֙ הַנָּבִ֔יא וַיַּעֲמֹ֥ד לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ עַל־הַדָּ֑רֶךְ וַיִּתְחַפֵּ֥שׂ בָּאֲפֵ֖ר עַל־עֵינָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: So the prophet went, and met the king in the way, and disguised himself by sprinkling dust on his face and his eyes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin propheta meni pois ja seisoi tiellä kuningasta vastassa, ja muutti kasvonsa tuhalla.
verses.king_james: So the prophet departed, and waited for the king by the way, and disguised himself with ashes upon his face.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque rex transisset, clamavit ad regem, et ait : Servus tuus egressus est ad præliandum cominus : cumque fugisset vir unus, adduxit eum quidam ad me, et ait : Custodi virum istum : qui si lapsus fuerit, erit anima tua pro anima ejus, aut talentum argenti appendes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֤י הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ עֹבֵ֔ר וְה֖וּא צָעַ֣ק אֶל־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַיֹּ֜אמֶר עַבְדְּךָ֣ ׀ יָצָ֣א בְקֶרֶב־הַמִּלְחָמָ֗ה וְהִנֵּֽה־אִ֨ישׁ סָ֜ר וַיָּבֵ֧א אֵלַ֣י אִ֗ישׁ וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ שְׁמֹר֙ אֶת־הָאִ֣ישׁ הַזֶּ֔ה אִם־הִפָּקֵד֙ יִפָּקֵ֔ד וְהָיְתָ֤ה נַפְשְׁךָ֙ תַּ֣חַת נַפְשׁ֔וֹ א֥וֹ כִכַּר־כֶּ֖סֶף תִּשְׁקֽוֹל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And as the king passed by, he cried to the king, and said: Thy servant went out to fight hand to hand: and when a certain man was run away, one brought him to me, and said: Keep this man: and if he shall slip away, thy life shall be for his life, or thou shalt pay a talent of silver.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin kuningas siitä kävi ohitse, huusi hän kuningasta ja sanoi: palvelias meni keskelle sotaa, ja katso mies pakeni ja johdatti miehen minun tyköni, ja sanoi: kätke tämä mies! jos niin on, että hän kaiketikin tulee pois, niin pitää sinun sielus oleman hänen sielunsa siassa, eli sinun pitää punnitseman sen edestä leiviskän hopiaa.
verses.king_james: And as the king passed by, he cried unto the king: and he said, Thy servant went out into the midst of the battle; and, behold, a man turned aside, and brought a man unto me, and said, Keep this man: if by any means he be missing, then shall thy life be for his life, or else thou shalt pay a talent of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum autem ego turbatus huc illucque me verterem, subito non comparuit. Et ait rex Israël ad eum : Hoc est judicium tuum, quod ipse decrevisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י עַבְדְּךָ֗ עֹשֵׂ֥ה הֵ֛נָּה וָהֵ֖נָּה וְה֣וּא אֵינֶ֑נּוּ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֵלָ֧יו מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל כֵּ֥ן מִשְׁפָּטֶ֖ךָ אַתָּ֥ה חָרָֽצְתָּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And whilst I, in the hurry, turned this way and that, on a sudden he was not to be seen. And the king of Israel said to him: This is thy judgment, which thyself hast decreed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska sinun palveliallas siellä ja täällä tekemistä oli, niin ei hän enää siellä ollut. Niin sanoi Israelin kuningas hänelle: sinun tuomios on oikia, sinä olet itse sen sanonut.
verses.king_james: And as thy servant was busy here and there, he was gone. And the king of Israel said unto him, So shall thy judgment be; thyself hast decided it.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille statim abstersit pulverem de facie sua, et cognovit eum rex Israël, quod esset de prophetis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְמַהֵ֕ר וַיָּ֙סַר֙ אֶת־הָ֣אֲפֵ֔ר (מעל) [מֵעֲלֵ֖י] עֵינָ֑יו וַיַּכֵּ֤ר אֹתוֹ֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל כִּ֥י מֵהַנְּבִיאִ֖ים הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he forthwith wiped off the dust from his face, and the king of Israel knew him, that he was one of the prophets.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän otti kohta tuhan pois kasvoistansa; ja Israelin kuningas tunsi hänen olevan prophetoita.
verses.king_james: And he hasted, and took the ashes away from his face; and the king of Israel discerned him that he was of the prophets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ait ad eum : Hæc dicit Dominus : Quia dimisisti virum dignum morte de manu tua, erit anima tua pro anima ejus, et populus tuus pro populo ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֗יו כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה יַ֛עַן שִׁלַּ֥חְתָּ אֶת־אִישׁ־חֶרְמִ֖י מִיָּ֑ד וְהָיְתָ֤ה נַפְשְׁךָ֙ תַּ֣חַת נַפְשׁ֔וֹ וְעַמְּךָ֖ תַּ֥חַת עַמּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to him: Thus saith the Lord. Because thou hast let go out of thy hand a man worthy of death, thy life shall be for his life, and thy people for his people.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi hänelle: näin sanoo Herra: ettäs päästit minulta kirotun miehen kädestäs, pitää sinun sielus oleman hänen sielunsa edestä ja sinun kansas pitää oleman hänen kansansa edestä.
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Because thou hast let go out of thy hand a man whom I appointed to utter destruction, therefore thy life shall go for his life, and thy people for his people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversus est igitur rex Israël in domum suam, audire contemnens, et furibundus venit in Samariam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֧לֶךְ מֶלֶךְ־יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל עַל־בֵּית֖וֹ סַ֣ר וְזָעֵ֑ף וַיָּבֹ֖א שֹׁמְרֽוֹנָה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the king of Israel returned to his house, slighting to hear, and raging came into Samaria.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Israelin kuningas meni vihaisena ja pahalla mielellä huoneesensa ja tuli Samariaan.
verses.king_james: And the king of Israel went to his house heavy and displeased, and came to Samaria.
